<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>NACA 2412 Airfoil CFD Analysis (ANSYS)</title>
<style>
  body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; max-width: 960px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 24px; color: #222; line-height: 1.6; }
  h1 { font-size: 2em; border-bottom: 2px solid #444; padding-bottom: 8px; }
  h2 { font-size: 1.4em; border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc; padding-bottom: 4px; margin-top: 2em; }
  h3 { font-size: 1.1em; margin-top: 1.5em; }
  table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin: 12px 0; }
  th, td { border: 1px solid #ccc; padding: 8px 12px; text-align: left; }
  th { background: #f0f0f0; font-weight: bold; }
  img { max-width: 100%; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px; margin: 8px 0; }
  .img-caption { font-size: 0.9em; color: #555; font-style: italic; margin-top: 4px; margin-bottom: 16px; }
  code { background: #f5f5f5; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 3px; font-size: 0.9em; }
  pre { background: #f5f5f5; padding: 16px; border-radius: 4px; overflow-x: auto; }
  blockquote { border-left: 4px solid #ccc; margin: 0; padding-left: 16px; color: #555; }
  .img-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 16px; margin: 16px 0; }
  @media (max-width: 600px) { .img-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }
</style>
</head>
<body>

<h1>NACA 2412 Airfoil CFD Analysis (ANSYS)</h1>
<p>CFD study of a <strong>NACA 2412</strong> airfoil at <strong>0° and 8° angle of attack</strong>, using ANSYS with the <strong>SST k-ω</strong> turbulence model on a structured C-domain mesh. Airfoil coordinates were sourced from <a href="http://airfoiltools.com/">Airfoil Tools</a> and the geometry, meshing, and post-processing were all done within ANSYS Workbench.</p>

<hr>

<h2>1. Overview</h2>
<p>This repository contains the setup, meshing strategy, boundary conditions, and results for a steady, incompressible RANS simulation of a NACA 2412 airfoil. The goal was to visualize and compare the flow field (velocity/pressure distribution) around the airfoil at two angles of attack: 0° (baseline) and 8°.</p>

<h2>2. Geometry</h2>
<table>
  <tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr>
  <tr><td>Airfoil</td><td>NACA 2412</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Coordinate source</td><td>airfoiltools.com (200-point coordinate file, CSV)</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Chord length</td><td>1 m</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Domain shape</td><td>C-shaped far-field domain (better wake/accuracy capture than a plain rectangular domain)</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Leading-edge enclosure</td><td>C-shape: R = 7.5 m, L = 15 m, H = 15 m</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Trailing edge treatment</td><td>Vertical split line added at the trailing edge for cleaner mesh transition in the wake region</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Geometry split</td><td>Divided into 6 regions to allow structured/quad-dominant meshing</td></tr>
</table>

<h2>3. Mesh</h2>
<ul>
  <li>Domain split into <strong>6 regions</strong>, each meshed independently for better control and quality.</li>
  <li><strong>Bias factor of 50,000</strong> applied to the edges near the airfoil surface to heavily cluster cells close to the airfoil (capturing steep near-wall gradients).</li>
  <li>Vertical trailing-edge split line used specifically to improve mesh quality/transition in that region.</li>
</ul>

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="C-domain mesh overview">
<p class="img-caption">Figure 1: C-domain mesh — structured quad cells near the airfoil transitioning to unstructured triangular elements in the wake region.</p>

<h2>4. Solver Setup</h2>
<table>
  <tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr>
  <tr><td>Solver</td><td>ANSYS Fluent</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Turbulence model</td><td>SST k-ω</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Simulation type</td><td>Steady-state</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Wall friction</td><td>Not considered in this analysis</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Freestream (inlet) velocity</td><td>45.6 m/s</td></tr>
  <tr><td>Reynolds number</td><td>3.1 × 10⁶ (based on 1 m chord)</td></tr>
</table>

<h2>5. Case Parameters</h2>
<p>The inlet velocity was decomposed into x and y components based on angle of attack (Vx = V·cos α, Vy = V·sin α).</p>
<table>
  <tr><th>Angle of Attack</th><th>Vx (m/s)</th><th>Vy (m/s)</th><th>Reynolds No.</th></tr>
  <tr><td>0°</td><td>45.6</td><td>0</td><td>3.1 million</td></tr>
  <tr><td>8°</td><td>45.156</td><td>6.34</td><td>3.1 million</td></tr>
</table>

<h2>6. Methodology / Workflow</h2>
<ol>
  <li>Downloaded the 200-point NACA 2412 coordinate file (CSV) from airfoiltools.com.</li>
  <li>Imported the CSV into ANSYS (DesignModeler/SpaceClaim) and scaled the profile to a 1 m chord.</li>
  <li>Built a C-shaped far-field enclosure around the airfoil (R = 7.5 m, L = 15 m, H = 15 m).</li>
  <li>Split the geometry into 6 regions and added a vertical line at the trailing edge to improve local mesh quality.</li>
  <li>Meshed each region, applying a bias factor of 50,000 on edges near the airfoil to refine the boundary layer region.</li>
  <li>Set up the case in Fluent: steady, pressure-based solver, SST k-ω turbulence model.</li>
  <li>Applied a velocity inlet of 45.6 m/s, resolved into Vx/Vy components for the 8° case.</li>
  <li>Ran simulations for α = 0° and α = 8°.</li>
  <li>Post-processed and visualized the velocity and pressure flow fields for both cases.</li>
</ol>
<blockquote><strong>Note / limitation:</strong> Wall friction (viscous shear at the airfoil surface) was not accounted for in this analysis.</blockquote>

<h2>7. Results</h2>

<h3>7.1 α = 0° — Zero Angle of Attack (Baseline)</h3>

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Velocity contour — 0°">
<p class="img-caption">Figure 2: Velocity magnitude contour at α = 0°. Flow is nearly symmetric about the chord line. Peak velocity (~56 m/s) occurs at the leading-edge shoulders where streamlines accelerate around the airfoil.</p>

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Pressure contour — 0°">
<p class="img-caption">Figure 3: Static pressure contour at α = 0°. High stagnation pressure at the leading edge (red ~1,270 Pa) and symmetric low-pressure regions on both surfaces. Small residual low-pressure zone at the trailing edge.</p>

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Velocity vectors — 0°">
<p class="img-caption">Figure 4: Velocity vectors colored by magnitude at α = 0°. The flow follows the airfoil surface cleanly with no separation, and a thin wake trails from the trailing edge.</p>

<h3>7.2 α = 8° — Eight Degree Angle of Attack</h3>

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Velocity contour — 8°">
<p class="img-caption">Figure 5: Velocity magnitude contour at α = 8°. The upper surface shows a pronounced high-velocity region (peak ~89 m/s) near the leading edge as flow accelerates over the cambered suction side. Lower surface velocity is noticeably reduced.</p>

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Pressure contour — 8°">
<p class="img-caption">Figure 6: Static pressure contour at α = 8°. Strong low-pressure region (blue, down to −4,110 Pa) on the upper surface drives lift. The lower surface retains higher pressure, creating the net upward force.</p>

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Velocity vectors — 8°">
<p class="img-caption">Figure 7: Velocity vectors colored by magnitude at α = 8°. The upwash ahead of the leading edge and downwash behind the trailing edge are clearly visible. Flow deflects downward as it leaves the airfoil, consistent with lift generation.</p>

<h2>8. Repository Structure</h2>
<pre>
.
├── README.md
├── geometry/        # Airfoil CSV, geometry files
├── mesh/            # Mesh files / screenshots
├── results/         # Result files
└── images/          # Result/flow visualization images
</pre>

<h2>9. Tools Used</h2>
<ul>
  <li>ANSYS Workbench (Geometry, Meshing, Fluent) — 2025 R2</li>
  <li>Airfoil coordinates: <a href="http://airfoiltools.com/">airfoiltools.com</a></li>
</ul>

<h2>10. Credits</h2>
<ul>
  <li>NACA 2412 airfoil coordinate data: Airfoil Tools (<a href="http://airfoiltools.com/">airfoiltools.com</a>)</li>
</ul>

</body>
</html>
